titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Kohar Alexanian Papers
titlestmt.author: Michael L. Forstrom

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday November 27, 2024 at 23:24

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James Arsenault & Company on the Jockey Hollow Fund, 2022.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Artwork, circa 1971-1979. II. Correspondence, 1968-1981. III. Printed Materials, 1941-1981. IV. Other Papers, 1981-1999.

bioghist.head: Kohar Alexanian
bioghist.p: Kohar Alexanian, designer and art director in the Juvenile Books Group at Harper & Row Publishers.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Kohar Alexanian Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Kohar Alexanian papers consist of artwork, correspondence, printed materials, and other papers documenting Alexanian's professional activities as a designer and art director at Harper & Row Publishers from the late 1960s to early 1980s. The papers feature original artwork and correspondence from authors and illustrators of well-known and award-winning children's books. Contributors include Marie Angel, Sandra Boynton, Palmer Brown, Malcolm Carrick, Patricia Collins, Carolyn Croll, Whitney Darrow, Mayling Mack Holm, Howard Knotts, R. R. Knudson, Anita and Arnold Lobel, Myra McGee, Allen Say, Fritz Siebel, Marc Simont, James Stevenson, Dominique Michele Strandquest, and Sally Wittman.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Kohar Alexanian Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by creator and title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I. includes original artwork by Kohar Alexanian and authors and illustrators published by Harper & Row Publishers during the 1970s. Alexanian's work includes manuscript dummies for two unpublished stories for children and several ink and pencil drawings. There is also original work by Sandra Boyton, Palmer Brown, Malcolm Carrick, Arnold Lobel, Myra McGee, Leonard Shortall, and Dominique Michel Strandquest. Work includes simple pencil and pen and ink drawings, as well as ink and watercolor.

did.unittitle: "Kim and Kiss together," manuscript dummy of a story about a girl and her dog, with text and drawings, pencil
A Sound of chariots , printed illustrations
Untitled manuscript dummy of a story about a brother and sister, with pencil drawings and text, typescript, pasted in
Other drawings, ink and color pencil
Hester in the wild , cover art, ink and watercolor
Today is Shrew's day , cover art, ink and watercolor
A Birthday for the princess , [proofs?] of two illustrations
Frog and toad together in "The Garden," sketch, pen and ink over pencil, inscribed
Mouse tales , drawing, pencil, signed and inscribed
Untitled drawing, pencil and watercolor, possibly relating to Mouse tales
Lester and mother , sketches, pen and ink
Untitled sketch of a horse and chicken in a barn, pen and ink, possibly relating to The Wonderful world of horses
Maximus , drawing, ink and watercolor, annotated
Untitled drawings relating to Thumbelina, ink, color pencil, and watercolor
Untitled sketch of a boy in a meadow with a large, crowned pink bird, ink and watercolor
Untitled sketch of a circus scene, pastel on black paper
Untitled sketch (print?) of a girl sitting in the grass with a cat

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II. consists chiefly of letters and cards to Alexanian from authors and illustrators of children's books and memos from colleagues at Harper & Row Publishers. Correspondents and collaborators include: Marie Angel, Palmer Brown, Patricia Collins, Carolyn Croll, Whitney Darrow, Jr., Judy Glasser, Mayling Mack Holm, Eros Keith, Howard Knotts, R. R. Knudson, Anita and Arnold Lobel, Joan Sandin, Allen Say, Marc Simont, Sally Wittman, and Ann Zwinger. The bulk of the collaborators thank Alexanian for her work on specific titles, with some also highlighting the positive contributions she made to their projects. A few letters concern events and other matters. Colleagues include Barbara Bottner and Dorothy M. Hagan, among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes internal memos to Hagen from colleagues at Harper & Row Publishers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes four pieces of correspondence from Ann Zwinger to Lucille Schultz of the Juvenile Books Department, Harper & Row, Publishers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III. consists chiefly of printed materials documenting Alexanian's work as a designer and art director for children's books at Harper & Row Publishers during the 1970s. Book and exhibition catalogs, clippings, posters, and ephemera document book projects Alexanian contributed to and authors with whom she collaborated. Authors identified here include Myron Levoy, Anita and Arnold Lobel, and Dominique Michele Strandquest. Books include well-known titles such as Arnold Lobel's Frog and toad together (1972).

In addition to these work-related materials, there is a June 1943 "School life issue" from Central High School, [Binghamton, New York?], in which Kouhar ("Ko") Alexanian is noted for her arts and crafts interests and art awards.
Additional clippings can be found in Series IV. Other Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series IV. consists of a small group of diverse material, featuring a folder ("Art") with color studies, possibly by Alexanian.

did.unittitle: 1981 American Library Association annual meeting schedule

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes color studies and charts, watercolor, and clippings

did.unittitle: Color swatches, watercolor, removed from "Art" folder
"Color swatches for jacket of The Wonderful world of horses "
Photograph of Kohar Alexanian and Arnold Lobel by Helen Marcus
